behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler + signature verification + enqueue handoff. A pure Unit test of verifier logic would miss wiring errors (e.g., handler still forwarding on auth failure). Rejected System because behavior text is about auth/forwarding logic, not real network/OS effects; in-process HTTP/module wiring is sufficient. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with in-process HTTP test harness, send body/header with invalid HMAC, assert 401 and assert queue write was never called/persisted.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue). Unit tests of either side alone can pass while ordering is wrong. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; ordering can be validated with in-process server + real queue module/local persistence. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument queue write completion and HTTP response timing in an integration test; assert write commit happens before 200 is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is async orchestration between request path and downstream worker trigger. Unit tests of isolated functions can miss await/Promise wiring mistakes. Rejected System because behavior text concerns waiting semantics, which can be proven with in-process wiring by stalling downstream and observing immediate response.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP test; make downstream processor block (unresolved promise/sleep), verify handler responds 200 before processor completes.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires wall-clock SLA under concurrency; real scheduling/event-loop/HTTP stack effects can hide in mocks or fake timers. Mocked/in-process shortcuts can miss latency regressions that appear only with real runtime and concurrent load. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start real service instance, create concurrent load with intentionally slow downstream workers on earlier events, send new webhook over real HTTP, measure end-to-end latency and assert <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at handoff/state boundary (handler + dedup store/queue persistence). Unit tests of ID-check helper miss persistence/transaction wiring bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is about duplicate-state correctness, not real OS/network behavior; local DB/filesystem-backed integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Submit same event ID twice through handler in integration environment with real dedup persistence; assert only one queue record exists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is translation of queue-capacity signal to HTTP status/headers across module boundary. Unit test of mapping function is insufficient if wiring/error propagation is broken. Rejected System because behavior text says 'queue reports it is full' (signal handling), which can be reproduced in-process without real network infra. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Force queue layer to return/full-error in integration test path; assert HTTP 503 and correct Retry-After header value.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue + persistence behavior, beyond handler logic. A mocked queue would always pass and hide real storage/capacity enforcement bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is local persistence capacity enforcement, typically testable with real queue module and local DB/filesystem in-process; no required external OS/network primitive beyond integration scope. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with small configured capacity in integration environment using real persistence backend; enqueue up to limit, then assert next enqueue is rejected with capacity error.
